pages.full_text:
skyline on west side of L. Coniston, slate, bare volcanic

skyline on east side of L. Coniston, limestone, trees
pages.route_sketch_description: A hand-drawn horizon/skyline sketch spanning the page, showing two contrasting profiles labelled left and right. Left section captioned 'skyline on west side of L. Coniston, slate, bare volcanic'; right section captioned 'skyline on east side of L. Coniston, limestone, trees'. The sketch illustrates the geological difference between the two sides of Coniston Water.
pages.notes: The page contains a panoramic skyline sketch of Coniston Water (Lake District) rather than a map, but it functions as an observational diagram. No handwritten page number is visible in the top-left corner. There is a small amount of text at the very bottom-right of the image that is partially cut off and unreadable.

pages.full_text:
Dry stone wall at Coniston. Alternating layers of flat slates & round limestone.

Sheep. oats original crops.

Bee skeps made out of rye + split bramble.

        The Gondola brought us back. I have never been in such a comfortable, fast, quiet small boat. The first passenger trip isn't until 11, but the men arrive at 9.0. to start the fire.
pages.notes: Page contains two hand-drawn sketches: (1) a detailed drawing of a dry stone wall labelled as being at Coniston, showing alternating layers of flat slates and round limestone; (2) two sheep sketches. The Gondola is the Victorian steam yacht on Coniston Water, owned by the National Trust. No year is stated on this page; year cannot be determined from this spread alone.
